description_long: This is a gold-colored ring featuring an openwork design with repeating interlaced triquetra (Celtic knot) motifs encircling the entire band. The ring is constructed with two parallel, smooth bands at the top and bottom, connected by the intricate knotwork, giving it a lightweight and airy appearance. The triquetra patterns are evenly spaced and symmetrically arranged, creating a harmonious and balanced visual effect. The surface of the ring appears highly polished, contributing to its glossy and reflective finish. There are no visible gemstones or additional embellishments, emphasizing the geometric and symbolic nature of the design. The overall craftsmanship suggests a piece intended for both aesthetic appeal and symbolic significance.
description: A gold ring with openwork Celtic knot motifs and a polished, glossy finish. The design is intricate, symmetrical, and free of gemstones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical arrangement of the triquetra motifs and the polished gold bands.
description_view_1: Side view with a clear display of the repeating knotwork pattern and the open spaces between motifs.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, highlighting the continuous geometric motif around the band.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the openwork design and the smooth, reflective gold surface.
